name: Alec Wilder
abstract: Alexander Lafayette Chew Wilder  (February 16, 1907 – December 24, 1980) was an American composer, author, and radio host. Considered one of the most inventive and lyrical American composers, he wrote thousands of pieces of music across various genres, including American popular song and classical music.

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: He was a very good friend and a lot of fun. ... he used to write all kinds of pieces for his friends, so he wrote several pieces for me which I recorded.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, April 26, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: He and I were always hanging out at the Cookery... Alec said to me, 'If you would record Jimmie Rowles, I’ll give you $1,000 towards the date.'
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: Later on, I did an Alec Wilder record a couple years ago... The record I did, I did with trio... We did Kurt Weill and Alec.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie Cain and Roy Kral
about_quotes.evidence: we meet him, and we talked and finally he said well do a couple of things... we did this one song... Alec sat down in a sofa with his back to us... he said, 'well Roy changes my chords but usually they’re for the better.'
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie Cain and Roy Kral interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, March 22, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Buddy Collette
about_quotes.evidence: And I also heard Julius Baker and he did something of Alec Wilder’s. It was where I think Alec did this piece...
about_quotes.doc_title: Buddy Collette interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 13, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Rufus Reid
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] I think it was Marian McPartland who said, via Alec Wilder, “I’d kill for a good bass line.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Rufus Reid interviewed by Monk Rowe, Atlanta, GA, January 4, 2013
